page: Amazon Web Services r5.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance powered by the AWS Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 24 physical cores and 48 vCPUs running at 3.1 GHz. The instance is equipped with 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, providing a memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps.

Benchmark results place the r5.12xlarge consistently in the average performance tier across multiple workloads. The Intel Xeon processor achieves a Geekbench single-core score of 1151.0 and a multi-core score of 10528.0. Memory performance is characterized by a read-only small block cached bandwidth of 2252.86 GB/sec and an uncached large block read bandwidth of 110.33 GB/sec. In database-specific tests, the instance processes 37.65 million Redis SET operations per second and scores 15620.63 on PassMark database operations.

The lack of local NVMe storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which may impact I/O-heavy workloads. Additionally, single-threaded performance is a relative bottleneck, as evidenced by a weak rating in single-threaded gzip compression at 23.35 GB/sec. However, the high memory capacity and solid multi-core capabilities make this instance well-suited for memory-intensive applications, including in-memory databases, caching layers, large-scale data processing, and enterprise applications that require a high memory-to-CPU ratio without the need for hardware acceleration.
description: Amazon Web Services r5.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs and 24 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor running at 3.1 GHz. Built on the AWS Nitro hypervisor, it delivers 384.0 GB of DDR4 memory at 3 GHz, establishing a high memory-to-vCPU ratio of 8 GB per vCPU. The instance lacks local storage and GPU accelerators, relying on a baseline network bandwidth of 12 Gbps. Benchmark data indicates average performance across most metrics, including a multi-core Geekbench score of 10528.0 and a Redis SET throughput of 37.65 million operations per second. While single-threaded compression tasks show weaker relative performance, the substantial memory footprint and solid multi-threaded capabilities make the r5.12xlarge highly effective for memory-intensive workloads such as in-memory databases, caching layers, and large-scale data processing.
og_description: Amazon Web Services r5.12xlarge is a memory-optimized instance featuring 48 vCPUs, 384 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, designed for memory-intensive database and caching workloads.

tagline: A memory-optimized virtual server featuring 384 GB of DDR4 RAM and 48 vCPUs for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.

384.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz
